sections.heading: Sector 1
sections.body:
As you approach turn one, known as Brabham, ensure you position your car on the left side of the track, brake at the 100-meter board, downshift to 5th gear, and clip the inside kerb. A smooth exit is crucial for maintaining speed into turn two.

Stay in 6th gear through turn two and use the whole track width on exit to maximise acceleration and exit speed as you start to move over to the left again through the slight kink down into the hairpin at turn three.

Turn three, or Whiteford, is the slowest corner on the circuit. Brake at the one-hundred-meter board, downshift to 3rd gear, and be cautious with throttle application to avoid spinning the rear tyres up whilst at such low speeds.

Short burst into turn four — a light brake and downshift to 3rd gear. Cut the inside curb slightly to straighten the approach into turn five. That right-hander is flat out, so exit turn four cleanly to carry as much speed as possible. Be smooth on steering input and avoid jerky corrections here that could unsettle the car whislt relying on the downforce so heavily.

sections.heading: Sector 2
sections.body:
This medium-speed right-hander demands confidence. Brake gently while shifting to 4th gear, aiming to skim the inside curb. Avoid over-committing — the exit narrows, and its easy to drift wide. Exit speed is essential, as it leads into a fast, momentum-based sequence.

Fast and flowing, this kink into turn seven is flat, and the long turn eight is also entirely flat out after the reprofiling of this section of Albert Park.

sections.heading: Sector 3
sections.body:
The most iconic section of Albert Park. Cancel the DRS just before braking at the 50m board, drop to 6th gear, and turn right aggressively while mounting the inside kerb. Instantly flick left into turn ten and use plenty of kerb again. Full throttle on exit with precision — a slight lift may be needed in race trim.

After the fast chicane, keep left and brake gently into the 90-degree right turn. Shift down to 4th gear, aim for the apex, and apply throttle early. Use the kerb on exit, but dont exceed track limits. This leads to another DRS zone, so your exit here is crucial for maintaining top speed.

A slightly downhill left-hander. Brake early — its easy to overshoot. Drop to 3rd gear and use a little inside curb. Avoid mid-corner oversteer by being patient on the throttle. Let the car run wide on exit to maintain speed, but stay clean.

Brake hard again and shift to 2nd gear. This corner has a tight entry, so prioritise rotation. Use as much of the inside kerb as possible without unsettling the car. Smooth throttle on exit sets you up nicely for the final corner sequence.

The final corner, turn 14, is a slight lift or short brake, taken in 4th or 5th gear. Keep tight to the inside kerb and quickly flick left into turn fifteen — the final corner. Take this in 3rd or 4th gear, clip the inside, and get back on the throttle early to launch down the main straight. The DRS zone follows almost immediately.
